title: The MCP long tail is real: 1,500+ servers indexed
excerpt: Influzer.ai’s aggregated MCP catalog crossed 1,500 server listings — with daily tool validation on entries that expose indexed capabilities.
contentHtml:
Eighteen months ago, “MCP server” meant filesystem and GitHub demos. The catalog today spans databases, browsers, SaaS products, cloud providers, and niche dev utilities — 1,500+ listings in Influzer.ai’s aggregated directory, with weekly registry syncs and daily tool-list validation.

What changed:

  • Vendors ship first-party MCP endpoints alongside REST APIs
  • Community servers cover gaps faster than internal platform teams
  • IDE clients (Cursor, Claude Desktop) made “attach a server” a config problem, not a sprint

Why it matters: the build-vs-compose decision flipped. Searching the catalog first is often faster than writing another integration wrapper.

Explore the Top 100 or full directory. Missing a server? Submit it.
